fr.recommendations: Improving emergency response effectiveness at the property could include the management of dense vegetation to maintain clear and accessible routes for emergency personnel. This may enhance visibility and movement, ensuring more efficient navigation to all structures. Creating or enhancing a secondary access route to the rear of the property could facilitate quicker access for responders, offering alternative pathways in case primary routes are blocked or congested. Clear signage and delineation of pedestrian walkways might assist in directing emergency responders efficiently between structures. Additionally, clearly marked points of entry and exit around the multiple structures on the property may support swift and effective staging and operational activities. Coordinating these efforts collectively could enhance overall responder access and movement throughout the property.

wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space around the property by increasing vegetation separation near the structures and reducing the presence of vegetation adjacent to structures could enhance wildfire resilience. Managing the dense tree canopy to create breaks may help reduce fuel continuity and support mitigation efforts in the event of a wildfire. Expanding open spaces around the residence by managing vegetation between structures and along the vegetated perimeter can further bolster defensible space. Strategic thinning of the tree cluster could provide an effective buffer, and maintaining clearance would facilitate firefighter access. Long-term management of these areas may contribute to improved property preparedness and suppression support.
